question_text: A student constructs two circuits using identical 6V batteries. In Circuit A, a single 12Ω resistor is connected. In Circuit B, two 12Ω resistors are connected in series. Which statement correctly compares the current flowing through each circuit?

options.label: A
options.text: Circuit A has twice the current of Circuit B because the total resistance is half

options.label: B
options.text: Both circuits have the same current because they use the same battery voltage

options.label: C
options.text: Circuit B has greater current because resistors in series decrease total resistance

options.label: D
options.text: The current in each circuit depends only on the battery type, not the resistance

correct_answer: Circuit A has twice the current of Circuit B because the total resistance is half
explanation: Using Ohm's law (V = IR), we can calculate the current in each circuit. Circuit A: I = 6V ÷ 12Ω = 0.5A. Circuit B: resistors in series add up, so total resistance = 12Ω + 12Ω = 24Ω, giving I = 6V ÷ 24Ω = 0.25A. Circuit A has twice the current because it has half the total resistance. This shows that current is inversely proportional to resistance when voltage is constant.
